LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MAN CHUNG WONG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM THAT WAS NOT BASED ON FACTS THAT WERE OR COULD HAVE BEEN RAISED IN THE FAMILY LAW PROCEEDING. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MAN CHUNG WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. PROOF OF SERVICE WAS NOT PROVIDED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302: DEFENDANT MAN CHUNG WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 30-DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO FILE A NEW COMPLAINT STATING WHY IN HER VIEW WHAT IS FILED IN THE CIVIL COURT IS DIFFERENT FROM WHAT IS FILED IN THE FAMILY COURT AND WHY WHAT THE FAMILY COURT DECIDED DOES NOT PRECLUDE HER FROM GETTING THE RELIEF SHE IS SEEKING. MR. WHANG IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.
1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SO, UNSUK AN INDIVIDUAL AS TO DEFENDANT WONG, MAN CHUNG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MANY CHUNG WONG'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED FROM AUGUST 29, 2016 TO OCTOBER 3, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302. OPPOSITION TO BE FILED BY SEPTEMBER 22, 2016; REPLY TO BE FILED BY SEPTEMBER 26, 2016. THE PLAINTIFF WAS DIRECTED BY THE COURT TO LODGE COURTESY COPIES OF OPPOSITION PAPERS WHETHER IT WAS FILED OR REJECTED BY THE CLERK'S OFFICE. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2016 Continued To Dec-21-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Unsuk So appears as plaintiff/opposing party, In Propria Persona, assisted by her daughter Sarah So, who is also present

Attorney, Victor M. Hwang, Esq. (415-595-6071) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

Man Chung Wong, appears as defendant.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MAN CHUNG WONG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a claim that was not based on facts that were or could have been raised in the family law proceeding. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Continued To Oct-03-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - UNSOK SO, TEL. NO. 1-415-672-1414 PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER WITH THE HELP OF DAUGHTER SARAH SO INTERPRETING ON HER BEHALF;
ATTY. VICTOR M. HWANG APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. Defendant Many Chung Wong's demurrer to the first amended complaint is continued to October 3, 2016. Opposition to be filed by September 22, 2016; Reply to be filed by September 26, 2016. The Plaintiff was directed by the court to lodge courtesy copies of Opposition papers whether it was filed or rejected by the clerk's office. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR # 6576 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Continued To Jul-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Plaintiff Unsuk So telephone number 415-672-1414 in Propria Persona.
Victor Hwang of Law Office of Victor M. Hwang telephone number 415-505-6077 for Defendant Man Chung Wong.
Sara So (Plaintiff's Daughter) also present to assist with translation.

The parties informed the Court that the plaintiff's attorney was substituted out and the plaintiff is now self represented. The parties spoke of the Family Court matter and presented oral argument as to whether or not the parties have a right to proceed in Civil Court. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MAN CHUNG WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 30-days leave to amend for plaintiff to file a new complaint stating why in her view what is filed in the Civil Court is different from what is filed in the Family Court and why what the Family Court decided does not preclude her from getting the relief she is seeking. Mr. Whang is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
